setupGuide.notes: Placeholder for Phoenix Street Circuit — 81 laps / 301.4 km reproduced as 95 laps of Long Beach. Long Beach hosted the United States GP West 1976-1983 — the era's US street-race character; Adelaide already hosts this season's Australian GP.
AMS2's Interlagos 1991 is the same rebuilt layout the 1991 Brazilian GP ran (4.325 km); the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.

track.realVenue: Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari

setupGuide.notes: AMS2's Imola 1988 is the same Enzo e Dino Ferrari circuit the 1991 San Marino GP ran; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-matching layout.
AMS2's Azure Circuit is Monaco; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the period-correct 1988 layout.
AMS2's Montreal 1991 is the same Circuit Gilles Villeneuve the 1991 Canadian GP ran; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the period-correct layout.

Placeholder for Autódromo Hermanos Rodríguez — 67 laps / 296.2 km reproduced as 59 laps of Jacarepagua Historic. Jacarepaguá keeps the era's Latin-American GP venue character; from 1990 the Brazilian GP itself runs at Interlagos, so the Interlagos stand-ins would double-book the venue.

Placeholder for Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours — 72 laps / 307.5 km reproduced as 73 laps of Le Mans Circuit Bugatti. Keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue; the 1991 French GP was the first at Magny-Cours, which is not in AMS2.
AMS2's Silverstone 1991 is the exact layout the 1991 British GP ran; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
No 1991 Hockenheim layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-matching 1988 layout.
AMS2 ships the 2025 Hungaroring; the venue is real and largely unchanged in character since 1986, so the historical lap count is kept.
No 1991 Spa layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-adjacent 1993 layout.
AMS2's Monza 1991 is the exact layout the 1991 Italian GP ran; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
AMS2's Cascais 1988 is the Estoril circuit the 1991 Portuguese GP ran; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-matching layout.
AMS2's Barcelona 1991 is the exact layout the inaugural 1991 Spanish GP at Catalunya ran; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.
Suzuka appears in AMS2 as Kansai; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the modern GP layout.

AMS2's Adelaide 1988 is the same street circuit the 1991 Australian GP ran; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept — the 1991 race was famously stopped after 14 laps in torrential rain.
